back_translation: 'You shall make an altar for burning incense; of acacia wood you shall make it.'
'A cubit its length and a cubit its width; it shall be square; and two cubits its height. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.'
'You shall overlay it with pure gold — its top and its sides round about and its horns — and make a molding of gold around it.'
'Two gold rings you shall make for it below its molding, on its two opposite sides; make them on its two sides, to serve as holders for poles to carry it.'
'You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.'
'You shall place it before the veil that is before the ark of the Testimony — before the mercy seat that is over the Testimony — where I will meet with you there.'
'Aaron shall burn on it fragrant incense; morning by morning, when he tends the lamps, he shall burn it.'
'When Aaron sets up the lamps between the evenings, he shall burn it — a continual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.'
'You shall not offer on it unauthorized incense, or a burnt offering, or a grain offering; nor shall you pour a drink offering on it.'

'Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once a year; with the blood of the sin offering of atonement, once a year he shall make atonement on it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the LORD.'
The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'When you take a census of the sons of Israel, each shall give a ransom for his life to the LORD when they are counted, so that there will be no plague among them when they are counted.'
'This is what everyone counted shall give: a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel — twenty gerahs to a shekel. A half shekel is a contribution to the LORD.'
'Everyone twenty years old and upward who is counted shall give the contribution to the LORD.'
'The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than the half shekel, when giving the contribution to the LORD to make atonement for your lives.'

'You shall take the atonement silver from the sons of Israel and use it for the service of the Tent of Meeting. It shall be for the sons of Israel a memorial before the LORD to make atonement for your lives.'
The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'You shall make a basin of bronze and its stand of bronze for washing, and you shall place it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water there.'
'Aaron and his sons shall wash from it their hands and their feet.'
'When they enter the Tent of Meeting, they shall wash with water and not die; or when they approach the altar to minister, to burn a food offering to the LORD,'
'they shall wash their hands and their feet, and not die. This shall be a perpetual statute for them — for him and for his descendants throughout their generations.'
The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
'You, take for yourself the finest spices: 500 shekels of liquid myrrh, half that amount (250) of fragrant cinnamon, and 250 of fragrant cane,'
'and 500 of cassia, according to the sanctuary shekel, and a hin of olive oil.'
'You shall make it a sacred anointing oil — a fragrant compound, the work of a perfumer; it shall be a sacred anointing oil.'
'You shall anoint with it the Tent of Meeting and the ark of the Testimony,'
'and the table and all its utensils, and the lampstand and its utensils, and the altar of incense,'
'and the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and the basin and its stand.'
'You shall consecrate them, and they shall be most holy; whatever touches them shall be holy.'
'You shall anoint Aaron and his sons, and you shall consecrate them to serve me as priests.'
'And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: This shall be my sacred anointing oil throughout your generations.'
'It shall not be poured on the flesh of an ordinary man, and according to its formula you shall not make anything like it. It is holy; it shall be holy to you.'
'Anyone who compounds anything like it or puts any of it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'Take fragrant spices — stacte, onycha, and galbanum — fragrant spices and pure frankincense; in equal parts shall they be.'
'You shall make incense — a compound, the work of a perfumer — salted, pure, holy.'
'You shall beat some of it very fine and put some of it before the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you there. It shall be most holy to you.'
'The incense that you shall make — according to its formula you shall not make for yourselves; it shall be holy to you, for the LORD.'
'Anyone who makes anything like it to smell of it shall be cut off from his people.'
